Title or Description: 2024 General Election Results

Notes: Added during ELEC-013 T2 remedy-selection audit and reassigned to ELEC-015 during the July 6 2026 boundary split; public route is JavaScript-driven, so exact totals should be verified against a static certified-result source before publication.

Title or Description: 2024 General Election Results

Title or Description: 52 U.S.C. § 30101 - Definitions
Proposition Supported: Primary FECA definitions source for ELEC-012 because the FECA conforming-amendment rewrite uses the existing definitions section as the hook for authorizing amendment, artificial entity, covered election, covered election spending, covered transfer, foreign-influenced entity, and true-source definitions.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T4/T5 FECA drafting-conversion follow-up; use for FECA codification and definitions placement, while reserving legislative-counsel review of final paragraph numbering and cross-references.

Title or Description: 8 U.S.C. § 1401 - Nationals and citizens of United States at birth
Date: Text contains laws in effect on June 25, 2026
Proposition Supported: Primary statutory source for RIGHTS-003 because subsection (a) codifies birthright citizenship for a person born in the United States and subject to its jurisdiction, and for RIGHTS-002 because nationality and citizenship recognition must be coordinated with any comprehensive immigration reform.
Notes: Added during the July 6 2026 post-Barbara statutory-vulnerability and RIGHTS-002 issue-admission pass; use as the initial title 8 statutory hook before drafting or scoring.

Title or Description: What to know about the Southern Poverty Law Center
Proposition Supported: Source-development lead for DOJ-003 because it reports that DOJ indicted the Southern Poverty Law Center on fraud-related charges tied to informant payments, describes SPLC's denial and law-enforcement-sharing defense, and includes expert criticism that the prosecution uses an unusual nonprofit-fraud theory.
Notes: Added during the July 9 2026 SPLC manifestation update; use as reporting and routing support only until indictment, superseding indictment, docket entries, DOJ statements, SPLC filings, and any court rulings are reviewed.

Title or Description: Southern Poverty Law Center pleads not guilty in federal fraud case
Proposition Supported: Current procedural-posture source for DOJ-003 because it reports that SPLC pleaded not guilty to a superseding indictment, summarizes DOJ's donor-fraud allegations, SPLC's denial and vindictive-prosecution claim, and the reported October trial setting.
Notes: Added during the July 9 2026 SPLC manifestation update; verify against the official docket, arraignment minute entry, superseding indictment, defense filings, and trial-scheduling order before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: 1992 Debates
Proposition Supported: Primary debate-history source for ELEC-013 because it records Ross Perot's participation with Bill Clinton and George H. W. Bush in all three 1992 general-election presidential debates and James Stockdale's participation in the vice-presidential debate, with dates, sponsors, formats, transcripts, and viewership figures.
Notes: Added during the July 9 2026 Perot 1992 case-study queue update; use as the modern debate-access case-study anchor while avoiding a causal claim that debate inclusion alone produced Perot's vote share.

Title or Description: Federal Elections 92
Proposition Supported: Primary official-results source for ELEC-013 because the FEC reports Ross Perot's 1992 national popular-vote total and 18.91 percent vote share, providing the official election-result baseline for the Perot debate-access case study.
Notes: Added during the July 9 2026 Perot 1992 case-study queue update; use with CPD debate-history records and later 1996/post-2000 debate-criteria sources before publication-ready analysis.

Title or Description: Registering as a candidate
Proposition Supported: Primary FEC guidance source for ELEC-013 because it explains when a federal candidate must register and file reports after raising or spending more than $5,000 and identifies principal campaign committee, tax ID/bank account, ballot access, treasurer, and registration requirements.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 presidential-debate and ballot-access explanatory source-development pass; use for procedural mapping only and pair with FECA, FEC regulations, forms, advisory opinions, and current agency guidance before publication-ready legal claims.

Title or Description: Gaining ballot access
Proposition Supported: Primary FEC guidance source for ELEC-013 because it states that state law governs candidate ballot access and that presidential candidates must contact the Secretary of State or appropriate election office in each state and territory for ballot requirements.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 presidential-debate and ballot-access explanatory source-development pass; supports the state-by-state verification model and should be paired with each state office's current cycle guide before detailed instructions are published.

Title or Description: Combined Federal/State Disclosure and Election Directory
Proposition Supported: Primary FEC directory source for ELEC-013 because it provides election-office contact routes and links for the 50 states, District of Columbia, and territories, including offices responsible for candidates on ballot and election results.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 presidential-debate and ballot-access explanatory source-development pass; use as a source-route directory, not as a substitute for state-specific candidate guides, statutes, forms, deadlines, certification records, or litigation.

Title or Description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. fails to qualify for CNN's debate. It'll be a showdown between Biden and Trump
Proposition Supported: Modern debate-access comparator for ELEC-013 because it reports that CNN's 2024 presidential-debate criteria required ballot access in states totaling at least 270 electoral votes and 15 percent support in four reliable national polls, and that Kennedy failed to meet the ballot and polling benchmarks.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 presidential-debate and ballot-access explanatory source-development pass; use as current-event illustration and verify against CNN's original criteria and FEC complaint materials before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Kennedy says he has secured ballot access in enough states to win. That's not yet true
Proposition Supported: Modern ballot-access and debate-qualification comparator for ELEC-013 because it distinguishes claimed/submitted ballot access from state-certified ballot access and reports that CNN would count confirmed states for debate qualification.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 presidential-debate and ballot-access explanatory source-development pass; use to illustrate certification timing and verification risk, while relying on official state certifications for legal claims.

Title or Description: Constitution of the United States: A Transcription
Proposition Supported: Primary constitutional text source for ELEC-013 because Article I section 4 supplies congressional authority over the times, places, and manner of congressional elections, while Article II section 1 frames State authority and congressional timing authority for presidential elector appointment.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 initial drafting pass; use for constitutional-authority mapping before any claim that a candidate-access filing interface may reach presidential elector elections.

Title or Description: Arizona v. Inter Tribal Council of Arizona
Proposition Supported: Primary Elections Clause source for ELEC-013 because the Court upheld the NVRA Federal Form acceptance/use structure as an exercise of congressional power over congressional-election procedure, making it the closest federal-form analogy for a candidate-access interface.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 initial drafting pass from Justia Supreme Court text; replace or pair with official U.S. Reports pinpoints before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Burroughs v. United States
Proposition Supported: Primary presidential-election authority source for ELEC-013 because the Court recognized congressional power to protect presidential elections from corrupt influence while preserving the need to avoid direct interference with State elector-appointment choices.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 initial drafting pass from Justia Supreme Court text; use as authority support for narrow presidential-election integrity and transparency hooks, not as a blanket national ballot-access mandate.

Title or Description: National Mail Voter Registration Form
Proposition Supported: Federal form comparator for ELEC-013 because the EAC form pairs a national baseline form with State-specific instructions, supporting the draft's standardized filing interface plus State supplement model.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 initial drafting pass; use as an administrative analogy, not as direct authority for candidate ballot-access entitlement.

Title or Description: Election Forms and Materials
Proposition Supported: Federal form comparator for ELEC-013 because the FPCA and FWAB show a federal election-administration model that combines standard forms, State-specific instructions, registration, absentee-ballot request, and backup-ballot functions.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 initial drafting pass; use as an implementation analogue for a Federal access form and State instructions.

Authority / Publisher: Department of Justice Civil Rights Division
Title or Description: Voting Section
Proposition Supported: Enforcement-role comparator for ELEC-013 because DOJ's Voting Section enforces civil provisions of the Voting Rights Act, UOCAVA, NVRA, HAVA, Civil Rights Acts, and related election statutes, supporting DOJ rather than FEC enforcement for State administration duties.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 initial drafting pass; use to justify civil enforcement allocation, not to imply DOJ supervises FEC debate rules.

Title or Description: How to file a complaint with the FEC
Proposition Supported: FEC enforcement-jurisdiction source for ELEC-013 because the FEC explains that it administers and enforces Federal campaign-finance law but does not enforce every election-related law, supporting the draft's split between FEC debate-rule enforcement and DOJ State-administration enforcement.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 initial drafting pass; pair with 52 U.S.C. 30109 and current FEC regulations.

Title or Description: Georgia lawmakers approve new election rules that could impact 2024 presidential contest
Proposition Supported: State-law comparator for ELEC-013 because Georgia's bill included a cross-state-recognition approach granting presidential ballot access to a party qualified in at least 20 states or territories, illustrating a narrower alternative to a national entitlement.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 initial drafting pass; verify enacted text, effective date, and current codification before treating Georgia as an operative model.

Title or Description: It's Time to Fix the Rules for Third-Party Presidential Campaigns
Proposition Supported: Prior-proposal and problem-framing comparator for ELEC-013 because Randy Barnett and Lawrence Lessig describe the 50-state ballot-access burden, inconsistent thresholds and deadlines, and Better for America's legal challenge to independent presidential candidate access barriers.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 initial drafting pass; use as prior-proposal/problem comparator, not as authoritative law.

Title or Description: Bost v. Illinois State Board of Elections
Proposition Supported: Current judicial source for ELEC-013 because the Court held that a federal candidate has Article III standing to challenge rules governing vote counting in the candidate's election; supports candidate-standing and litigation-timing analysis while not deciding the merits of state ballot-receipt deadlines.
Notes: Added during the July 9 2026 T1 draft check; use for current-status and timing-risk analysis, not as authority that every candidate-access floor is valid.

Title or Description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. files complaint over rules for CNN's presidential debate next month
Proposition Supported: Modern debate-access and FEC-complaint comparator for ELEC-013 because it reports Kennedy's FEC complaint over CNN's June 2024 debate criteria, CNN's response, the 270-electoral-vote ballot-access and 15-percent polling thresholds, and the submitted-versus-confirmed ballot-access dispute.
Notes: Added during the July 9 2026 T2 development audit; use as a current-status comparator only and verify against the FEC complaint file, response, agency action, and any court record before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Running For President in Texas (in 2024)
Proposition Supported: Official state ballot-access sample for ELEC-013 because it identifies Texas presidential routes, independent-candidate filing deadline, 113151-signature requirement, voter-primary-disqualification rule for petition signers, vice-presidential consent, and 40 presidential-elector consent requirements.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 T3 legal-durability and implementation audit; use as a high-burden independent-presidential sample and pair with current-cycle Texas forms, code sections, cure/challenge rules, and certification records before publication-ready instructions.

Title or Description: Independent and Third Party Presidential Candidate Bulletin
Date: Sept. 2023 bulletin for Nov. 5 2024 election; accessed July 9, 2026
Proposition Supported: Official state ballot-access and cure/appeal sample for ELEC-013 because it sets out Virginia presidential petition, elector, filing, substitution, material-omission, signature-review, and appeal procedures, including 5000 statewide signatures, 200 per congressional district, 13 pledged electors, and a seven-day appeal route after petition disqualification.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 T3 legal-durability and implementation audit; use as a procedural model for notice, defect classification, and appeal design, not as a substitute for current-cycle Virginia forms or legal advice.

Title or Description: 2024 Nonpartisan Candidate Selection Criteria
Date: Adopted Oct. 30, 2023; announced Nov. 20, 2023; accessed July 9, 2026
Proposition Supported: Official debate-sponsor criteria source for ELEC-013 because CPD's 2024 criteria identify constitutional eligibility, ballot access sufficient for a mathematical Electoral College majority, and at least 15 percent national support across five selected national public-opinion organizations, while citing the FEC pre-established-objective-criteria rule.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 T4 pre-publication audit; use as an official sponsor-criteria comparator, not as proof that the criteria are lawful, optimal, or enforceable.

Title or Description: Statement on CPD's 2024 General Election Debates
Proposition Supported: Official debate-administration source for ELEC-013 because CPD released the four 2024 debate sites from their contracts after the Biden campaign declined CPD sponsorship, stated that CPD stood ready should circumstances change, and described its debate format and neutral-sponsor rationale.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 T4 pre-publication audit; use to show 2024 sponsor disruption and public-benefit/venue reliance issues, not to imply CPD unlawfulness or final FEC disposition.

Title or Description: H.R. 1503 - 111th Congress presidential eligibility documentation bill
Proposition Supported: Direct federal prior-proposal comparator for ELEC-013 because H.R. 1503 would have amended FECA to require a presidential candidate's principal campaign committee to include a birth certificate and other eligibility documentation with the committee's statement of organization.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 Review Ready source-development push; useful as a FECA/FEC candidate-documentation analogue but politically and substantively narrower than ELEC-013 and not a ballot-access or debate-access model.

Title or Description: H.R. 1 - For the People Act of 2019 committee print - Title X presidential and vice-presidential tax transparency
Proposition Supported: Direct federal prior-proposal comparator for ELEC-013 because Title X would have required covered presidential and vice-presidential candidates, Presidents, and Vice Presidents to submit income tax returns to the FEC and would have treated the returns as FECA reports after redaction.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 Review Ready source-development push; supports the FEC-facing candidate-disclosure analogue while showing that prior federal models concern disclosure rather than State ballot-access cure or debate transparency.

Title or Description: SB 27 - Primary elections: ballot access: tax returns
Proposition Supported: State prior-proposal and cautionary comparator for ELEC-013 because California SB 27 conditioned presidential primary ballot access on filing five years of income tax returns with the Secretary of State and posting redacted versions publicly, while declaring no appropriation and trivial compliance costs.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 Review Ready source-development push; use as a cautionary ballot-access disclosure comparator because later litigation invalidated the presidential application and because ELEC-013 avoids a state-imposed substantive presidential qualification.

Title or Description: H.R. 4228 - DHS Acquisition Accountability and Efficiency Act cost estimate
Date: May 21, 2014; reviewed as budget analogue July 9, 2026
Proposition Supported: Budget analogue for ELEC-013 because CBO estimated a procedural administrative, reporting, GAO-review, and inspector-general-review bill at $1 million in the first year and less than $500000 annually thereafter, illustrating the low end for oversight/reporting reforms without broad grants.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 Review Ready source-development push; direct CBO page retrieval was limited in the audit environment, so publication-ready reliance should verify the CBO page or House report text before quoting. Adjudicated 2026-07-18: Cite as ELEC-013 low-end administrative and reporting cost analogue. Integrated through areas/ELEC/issues/ELEC-013.md.

Title or Description: H.R. 9 - Innovation Act cost estimate
Date: July 10, 2015; reviewed as budget analogue July 9, 2026
Proposition Supported: Budget analogue for ELEC-013 because CBO estimated an administrative and judicial-procedure bill with new reports, rules, database/public-information work, and agency process changes at about $7 million per year over five fiscal years for several affected entities.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 Review Ready source-development push; direct CBO page retrieval was limited in the audit environment, so publication-ready reliance should verify the CBO page, House report, or official PDF before quoting. Adjudicated 2026-07-18: Cite as ELEC-013 rulemaking, database, and process cost analogue. Integrated through areas/ELEC/issues/ELEC-013.md.

Title or Description: Election officials in the US face daunting challenges in 2024. And Congress isn't coming to help
Proposition Supported: Budget ceiling comparator for ELEC-013 because AP reported that Congress had provided $995 million since 2016 for election-security enhancement and that the last significant tranche was $75 million in December 2022, illustrating that ELEC-013's limited administrative and technical-assistance posture is far below major election-security modernization scale.
Notes: Added during ELEC-013 Review Ready source-development push; use only as a scale comparator and obtain appropriations/EAC records before publication-ready fiscal reliance. Adjudicated 2026-07-18: Cite only as ELEC-013 upper-scale election-security funding contrast. Integrated through areas/ELEC/issues/ELEC-013.md.

Title or Description: FBI seizure of Georgia ballots reflects pursuit of Trump's 2020 election grievance
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for ELEC-014 because AP reported that FBI agents executed a search at Fulton County's election hub and seized hundreds of boxes containing 2020 ballots and other election documents while the warrant remained sealed and federal authorities had not publicly explained the basis.
Notes: Use to catalog the Georgia search/seizure manifestation; publication-ready reliance requires the warrant, affidavit, return, docket materials, court orders, and official DOJ/FBI explanation.

Title or Description: The FBI is directing hundreds of analysts to its probe of Georgia's 2020 presidential election
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for ELEC-014 because AP reported that the FBI directed 260 analysts and staff operations specialists to assist the Fulton County 2020 election investigation and review records after the January 2026 seizure of ballots and related documents.
Notes: Use to catalog investigation scale and record-review burden; verify against the FBI memo, any court filings, DOJ explanation, and congressional oversight materials before relying on precise operational details.

Title or Description: Judge rejects Justice Department attempt to get names of 2020 election workers in Fulton County
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for ELEC-014 because AP reported that a federal judge quashed an April 2026 grand-jury subpoena seeking names and personal contact information for all Fulton County employees and volunteer poll workers from the 2020 election.
Notes: Use to catalog the Georgia election-worker identity subpoena and judicial response; publication-ready reliance requires the subpoena, motion to quash, hearing transcript if available, and Judge William Ray's order.

Title or Description: The Justice Department seeks voter and election information from at least 19 states AP finds
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for ELEC-004 because AP reported DOJ civil/administrative requests for voter registration lists and election information from at least 19 states, including Colorado all-records language for 2024 and retained 2020 election records and information-sharing contacts involving a senior Criminal Division counsel.
Notes: Route primarily to ELEC-004's civil-process lane, with ELEC-001/ELEC-010 cross-reference for federal election-control and voter-data architecture. Do not treat as ELEC-014 criminal-process material unless a subpoena, warrant, grand jury, Criminal Division investigative channel, or prosecution threat is verified.

Title or Description: Justice Department sues six more states to get detailed voter data
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for ELEC-004 because AP reported that DOJ sued Delaware, Maryland, New Mexico, Rhode Island, Vermont, and Washington over statewide voter-registration lists, bringing its voter-data lawsuits to at least 14 states and its requests to at least 26 states.
Notes: Use as scale evidence for civil state-election-record litigation and voter-data demands; do not treat as ELEC-014 criminal process without separate criminal-process proof.

Title or Description: A federal judge dismisses another DOJ lawsuit seeking voter data this time in Massachusetts
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for ELEC-004 because AP reported that a federal judge dismissed DOJ's Massachusetts voter-roll lawsuit, that DOJ had sued at least 30 states and D.C., and that DOJ represented it intended to run unredacted voter-roll data through DHS SAVE.
Notes: Use for ELEC-004 state-record-demand scope and privacy/federalism concerns; verify against case filings and the cited court order before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Judges in Maine and Wisconsin dismiss Justice Department's attempts to force turnover of voter rolls
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for ELEC-004 because AP reported that federal judges in Maine and Wisconsin dismissed DOJ voter-roll lawsuits and that DOJ had sued at least 30 states plus D.C. for detailed voter data including dates of birth, addresses, driver-license numbers, and partial Social Security numbers.
Notes: Use as ELEC-004 litigation-status and state-record-demand scale evidence; verify individual case orders before citing as final legal authority.

Title or Description: The Trump administration is ramping up pressure on states to change election practices
Proposition Supported: Source-development record because AP reported July 2026 DOJ letters to all 50 states and D.C. warning election officials of possible criminal charges tied to noncitizen voting and calling for compliance information within five days. Potentially supports the episode and institutional question: Whether unrelated public-safety grants may be conditioned on state compliance with executive election directives without clear congressional authorization
Notes: Use as a prosecution-threat manifestation and adjacent pressure signal; obtain the DOJ letter text and state responses before publication-ready reliance. Migrated from completed media intake MEDIA-021; pending qualitative placement. Current route assessment: existing-manifestation; retrieve-notice-of-funding-opportunity. Already incorporated into federalism and election work; preserve as a high-value primary-document retrieval task.

Title or Description: Trump justice department seeks 2020 election records from Georgia county
Proposition Supported: Source-development lead for ELEC-004 because The Guardian reported that DOJ requested Fulton County 2020 election records after the Georgia State Election Board reopened related inquiries and sought DOJ assistance; also a sequence lead for ELEC-014 only if primary records connect the civil request to later criminal search activity.
Notes: Route the civil record-request aspect to ELEC-004; preserve ELEC-014 cross-reference only for the later criminal-process sequence. Verify against the DOJ letter, Fulton County response, Georgia State Election Board records, and any federal-court filings.

Title or Description: Sweeping review of grand jury presentations underway in Chicago following misconduct revelations
Proposition Supported: Adjacent-pattern source for ELEC-014 and DOJ-003 because AP reported that DOJ began reviewing more than 1,000 Illinois grand-jury presentations after misconduct revelations in the Broadview Six anti-ICE protest prosecution, including a prosecutor meeting with a grand juror outside proceedings and dissenting grand jurors being dismissed from participation.
Notes: Use as adjacent DOJ grand-jury-integrity evidence, not as an election-process manifestation. Verify against Judge April Perry's orders, the released grand-jury transcripts, dismissal filings, and any sanctions or OPR/OIG materials before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Donald Trump ousts election commission members in latest push to reshape US voting process
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because AP reported that President Trump removed Democratic EAC commissioners Thomas Hicks and Benjamin Hovland after Trump v. Slaughter, Republican commissioner Christy McCormick resigned, and Republican commissioner Donald Palmer had already stepped down, leaving the four-member EAC without commissioners.
Notes: REG-001 is the primary home for the independent-commission removal and vacancy-collapse manifestation. Cross-reference ELEC-001 for election-administration pressure implications and ELEC-013 for HAVA/EAC candidate-access administration implications. Verify with White House notices, EAC commissioner statements, Senate records, HAVA provisions, and any litigation before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Ex-NLRB member challenges Trump's firing
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because Politico reported that former NLRB member Gwynne Wilcox sued after President Trump removed her despite statutory limits on removing board members except for neglect of duty or malfeasance.
Notes: Use for the NLRB row in the REG-001 removal catalog; verify against Wilcox docket filings, NLRB statutory text, and subsequent Supreme Court or appellate orders before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Trump fires 2 Democrats on the Federal Trade Commission seeking more control over regulators
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because AP reported President Trump dismissed FTC commissioners Alvaro Bedoya and Rebecca Kelly Slaughter, creating the core pre-Slaughter manifestation and later removal-power test case.
Notes: Use with Trump v. Slaughter and FTC Act records; verify removal letters, litigation filings, and final Supreme Court disposition before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Ex-civil rights agency commissioner fired by Trump drops lawsuit in wake of Supreme Court ruling
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because AP reported Jocelyn Samuels dropped her lawsuit challenging removal from the EEOC after the Supreme Court expanded presidential authority over independent agencies; it also recaps removals of Democratic EEOC commissioners before their terms expired.
Notes: Use for the EEOC row in the REG-001 removal catalog; verify EEOC statutory provisions, removal letters, lawsuit filings, and post-Slaughter dismissal posture.

Title or Description: Leaders of two independent agencies reinstated by appeals court
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because the Washington Post reported D.C. Circuit reinstatement of NLRB member Gwynne Wilcox and MSPB chair Cathy Harris after President Trump sought to remove them, pending legal challenges to independent-agency removal limits.
Notes: Use for MSPB and NLRB litigation posture in the removal catalog; verify docket records, statutory text, and later Supreme Court/appellate developments.

Title or Description: Trump continues clearout of top US labor officials with fresh firing
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because The Guardian reported President Trump dismissed Susan Tsui Grundmann from the Federal Labor Relations Authority after earlier labor-board removals.
Notes: Use for the FLRA row in the REG-001 removal catalog; verify FLRA statutory protections, removal records, and any litigation before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Trump fires three consumer watchdog commissioners as he dismantles agency
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because Politico reported President Trump fired three Democratic Consumer Product Safety Commission commissioners amid broader agency-dismantling efforts.
Notes: Use for the CPSC row in the REG-001 removal catalog and analyze operational dismantling under REG-001's Tier Two lane. Verify CPSC statutory text, removal letters, litigation filings, and final appellate or Supreme Court posture.

Title or Description: Federal judge blocks Trump's firing of Consumer Product Safety Commission members
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because AP reported a federal judge ruled President Trump's dismissal of three Democratic CPSC members unlawful and ordered reinstatement, before later appellate or emergency developments required verification.
Notes: Use for protection/status notes in the CPSC row; verify district-court order, stay applications, and later appellate or Supreme Court orders.

Title or Description: Federal campaign finance agency won't have enough members to enforce the law
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because Politico reported FEC quorum loss after commissioner resignations and noted President Trump's earlier attempted removal of Ellen Weintraub, who had ceased participating and was no longer listed on the FEC website.
Notes: Use for FEC row in REG-001 removal catalog and cross-reference ELEC-012/ELEC-013 where FEC enforcement, advisory opinions, or debate-rule administration matter. Verify FEC records, resignation letters, Weintraub removal correspondence, and FECA provisions.

Title or Description: The head of the FEC is defying Trump's attempt to fire her
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because Business Insider reported Ellen Weintraub disputed President Trump's attempted removal from the Federal Election Commission and argued the action was not legally compliant.
Notes: Use only as secondary reporting for the FEC attempted-removal row; verify against Weintraub's letter, FEC records, FECA holdover provisions, and any litigation.

Title or Description: Federal judge blocks Trump's firing of two Democratic members of privacy oversight board
Proposition Supported: Source-development record for REG-001 because AP reported a federal judge blocked President Trump's firing of former PCLOB members Travis LeBlanc and Edward Felten after earlier resignation demands and reported terminations left the board without a quorum.
Notes: Use for the PCLOB row in the REG-001 removal catalog; verify resignation demands, removal notices, district-court order, appellate posture, and PCLOB quorum records before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Overview of Take Care Clause
Proposition Supported: Primary constitutional-analysis source for REG-001 because it explains that the Take Care duty encompasses powers Congress assigns to department heads and executive agencies as well as powers assigned directly to the President.
Notes: Use as the cross-tier constitutional starting point; pair with cases addressing removal, appointments, reviewability, statutory duties, and presidential inability to enact, amend, or repeal statutes.

Title or Description: What is the Impoundment Control Act and What is GAO's Role?
Proposition Supported: Official source for REG-001's use of impoundment law as a partial anti-nullification model because GAO explains that executive action or inaction delaying or withholding enacted funding can constitute an impoundment and must comply with statutory procedures and limits.
Notes: Use as an analogy for deliberate inaction as a disabling mechanism, not as proof that every personnel, vacancy, transfer, or operational decision is governed by the Impoundment Control Act.

Title or Description: Two US Justice Dept antitrust officials fired over merger controversy
Proposition Supported: Source-development lead for HOR-025 because Reuters reported the firing of Principal Deputy AAG Roger Alford and merger-enforcement chief Bill Rinner after internal conflict over the HPE-Juniper settlement, unusual leadership signatures, alleged retaliation, and questions about required disclosure of government communications concerning the settlement proposal.
Notes: Use as a monitoring and primary-source routing lead, not proof of corrupt influence. Obtain the settlement filings, communication disclosures, personnel records where lawfully available, congressional correspondence, and any judicial or inspector-general findings. Adjudicated 2026-07-18: Use with attribution for the reported Alford and Rinner removals and do not treat it as proof of improper influence. Integrated through framework/logs/HORIZON_SCAN_LOG.md.

Title or Description: Top Trump antitrust official leaves post following disputes over big mergers
Proposition Supported: Source-development lead for HOR-025 because AP reported Gail Slater's departure amid merger-enforcement disputes and quoted former deputy Roger Alford alleging that cases were being resolved through political connections and lobbyist influence rather than legal merits.
Notes: Treat Alford's statements as allegations requiring documentary corroboration. Verify Slater's departure records, subsequent leadership structure, merger-specific files, and any OIG or congressional response. Adjudicated 2026-07-18: Use with attribution for Slater departure context and expressly label Alford statements as allegations. Integrated through framework/logs/HORIZON_SCAN_LOG.md.

Title or Description: Warren Klobuchar lawmakers letter to DOJ Inspector General regarding the Antitrust Division
Proposition Supported: Primary congressional request asking the DOJ Inspector General to examine alleged political and lobbyist influence over Antitrust Division matters and identifying merger and monopolization cases for documentary follow-up.
Notes: Use as a detailed allegation and source-routing document, not proof of the asserted conduct. Monitor OIG acceptance, scope, records requests, interim communications, and final findings. Adjudicated 2026-07-18: Primary request to the DOJ Inspector General supports the candidate monitoring trigger and must remain characterized as a request. Integrated through framework/logs/HORIZON_SCAN_LOG.md.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Department of Justice Antitrust Division
Title or Description: Statement on the Closing of Its Investigation of the Merger of Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros.
Proposition Supported: Official DOJ explanation for closing a major merger investigation after an eight-month career-staff review involving more than two million documents; useful as the Department's stated legal and evidentiary position and as a comparator for reports that career investigators favored a challenge.
Notes: Do not infer corruption from disagreement between the official decision and reported staff views. Obtain any public staff analysis, leadership memoranda, state-enforcement filings, party submissions, and later judicial findings before assessing improper influence. Adjudicated 2026-07-18: Official DOJ statement supplies the executive-branch explanation and a necessary comparator to reported staff disagreement. Integrated through framework/logs/HORIZON_SCAN_LOG.md.

Title or Description: Executive Order 14238 - Continuing the Reduction of the Federal Bureaucracy
Proposition Supported: Primary executive source directing USAGM, IMLS, MBDA, FMCS, and other listed entities to eliminate nonstatutory functions and reduce statutory functions and personnel to the minimum required by law.
Notes: Use as the common directive for the new REG-001 Tier Two entries; do not treat the order alone as proof that statutory capacity was actually disabled.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia
Title or Description: Widakuswara v. Lake memorandum opinion
Proposition Supported: Primary judicial source reviewing the 2025 USAGM and VOA downsizing and finding deficiencies in the agency's analysis of how the reduced structure would perform statutory international-broadcasting duties.
Notes: Track appeal and final relief. Use for statutory-performance and reviewability analysis, not as a final universal judgment against all USAGM restructuring.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the First Circuit
Title or Description: Rhode Island v. Trump
Proposition Supported: Primary appellate source preserving preliminary relief for IMLS, MBDA, and FMCS that required restoration of employees and functions and barred measures preventing fulfillment of statutory obligations while permitting reasoned efficiency measures.
Notes: Use as the shared source for the IMLS-MBDA-FMCS cluster; monitor merits proceedings and any Supreme Court review.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. District Court for the District of Maryland
Title or Description: Maryland v. Corporation for National and Community Service memorandum opinion
Proposition Supported: Primary judicial source identifying AmeriCorps staff leave and RIF actions, more than 1,000 program closures, NCCC demobilization, and proposed agency elimination, and granting partial preliminary relief for program and member actions.
Notes: Distinguish relief concerning grants and NCCC members from the court's treatment of staff-reduction standing; monitor final merits and nationwide operational status.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia
Title or Description: United States Institute of Peace v. Jackson memorandum opinion
Proposition Supported: Primary district-court source holding USIP board removals and subsequent dismantling actions unlawful and describing staff termination, cessation of operations, and asset transfer following the DOGE-assisted takeover.
Notes: Pair with the D.C. Circuit stay and later merits posture; use USIP as a Tier One and Tier Two bridge rather than presenting the district-court judgment as finally controlling.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit
Title or Description: United States Institute of Peace v. Jackson order granting stay pending appeal
Proposition Supported: Primary appellate source staying the district-court USIP judgment after concluding that the Government was likely to succeed on its executive-power theory.
Notes: Use to state the contested appellate posture accurately; monitor the case held in abeyance and any controlling Supreme Court removal-power decision.

Title or Description: 22 U.S.C. § 1928f - Limitation on withdrawal from the North Atlantic Treaty
Proposition Supported: Primary enacted model prohibiting presidential suspension, termination, denunciation, or withdrawal from the North Atlantic Treaty absent two-thirds Senate consent or an act of Congress.
Notes: Use as the leading statutory analogue for HOR-026; separately test enforcement, standing, appropriations, constructive-withdrawal, and constitutional objections before generalizing the model.

Title or Description: 22 U.S.C. § 290c - Withdrawal from the World Health Organization on one-year notice
Proposition Supported: Primary organization-specific withdrawal rule reserving a United States right to withdraw from WHO on one year's notice while requiring full payment of current-fiscal-year financial obligations.
Notes: Use to distinguish express congressional withdrawal authorization and payment conditions from organizations whose organic or participation statutes do not specify withdrawal authority.

Title or Description: Withdrawal from International Agreements: Legal Framework the Paris Agreement and the Iran Nuclear Agreement
Proposition Supported: Congressional legal framework distinguishing treaties, congressional-executive agreements, sole executive agreements, and political commitments; surveying international-law withdrawal rules, historical practice, Goldwater v. Carter, standing, and political-question barriers.
Notes: Use as the initial doctrine map rather than as a substitute for organization-specific statutes, treaty text, executive notices, legislative history, or current litigation.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 2284 - Three-judge court; when required; composition; procedure
Proposition Supported: Primary statutory basis showing that Congress may require a three-judge district court and prescribing the baseline designation and single-judge procedure adapted by JUD-011 and the REG-001 and FUND-001 fallback routes. Official statute governing when Congress may require a three-judge court panel formation and the limits on single-judge relief.
Notes: Legislative counsel should verify every proposed departure and conforming amendment, including random national roster assignment, emergency preservation, and proposal-specific fallback assignment. Consolidated duplicate SRC-0662; Both standalone routes now require the initial judge to notify the chief circuit judge and allow only Rule 65(b) temporary relief before panel formation.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 1253 - Direct appeals from decisions of three-judge courts
Proposition Supported: Primary appellate-jurisdiction source providing direct Supreme Court appeal from specified three-judge-court injunction orders except as otherwise provided by law. Official default rule allowing direct Supreme Court appeal from specified injunction orders of congressionally required three-judge courts except as otherwise provided by law.
Notes: JUD-011, REG-001, and FUND-001 propose D.C. Circuit review and therefore require express statutory coordination and legislative-counsel review. Consolidated duplicate SRC-0663; FUND-001 expressly makes section 1253 inapplicable and uses D.C. Circuit review under sections 1291 and 1292 with certiorari under section 1254.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 292 - District-judge designation and assignment
Proposition Supported: Primary statutory source for temporary designation of district judges within and across circuits.
Notes: Use as the initial assignment hook; verify whether JUD-011's random national roster requires additional amendments to sections 291-296.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 1657 - Priority of civil actions
Proposition Supported: Primary statutory source supporting expedited consideration where federal statutory or constitutional rights and the factual context establish good cause.
Notes: Use as an expedition analogue, not as proof that JUD-011 or REG-001's proposed deadlines are operationally feasible.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 1407 - Multidistrict litigation
Proposition Supported: Primary structural comparator for a national judicial panel composed of designated circuit and district judges with shared administration and defined statutory authority.
Notes: Distinguish multidistrict transfer and pretrial coordination from JUD-011's original D.D.C. jurisdiction and merits adjudication.

Title or Description: 50 U.S.C. § 1803 - Designation of judges
Proposition Supported: Primary structural comparator for a standing specialized court composed of district judges designated from multiple circuits.
Notes: Use only for judicial-designation and shared-court structure; JUD-011 rejects FISA's secrecy, one-sided application, and substantive mission as models for ordinary civil litigation.

Title or Description: H.R. 1702 JUDGES Act
Proposition Supported: Official cost comparator estimating 2025 district-judge compensation of about $280,000, typical first-year administrative costs of about $760,000, and average annual operating costs of about $700,000 once established.
Notes: Comparator only. It does not score JUD-011's clerk staff, facilities, secure records, cybersecurity, special masters, emergency operations, or appellate workload.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 296 - Powers upon designation and assignment
Proposition Supported: Primary statutory source describing the judicial powers exercised by a judge designated and assigned to another court or circuit.
Notes: Read with section 292 and verify whether the proposed JUD-011 roster and fixed terms require additional designation authority.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 133 - Appointment and number of district judges
Proposition Supported: Primary statutory source listing 15 authorized district judgeships for the District of Columbia before the two additional judgeships proposed by JUD-011.
Notes: Confirm the enacted baseline and conforming amendment immediately before introduction because later judgeship legislation could change the number.

Title or Description: 5 U.S.C. chapter 7 - Judicial Review
Proposition Supported: Primary statutory source for ordinary agency-action review, including the right of review, reviewable actions, interim relief, and scope of review that REG-001 preserves rather than displaces.
Notes: Legislative counsel should test REG-001's independent cause of action, sovereign-immunity treatment, finality and reviewability rules, and coordination with sections 701 through 706.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 144 - Bias or prejudice of judge
Proposition Supported: Primary statutory source for party-filed judicial bias affidavits used by the proposed interbranch-review and REG-001 panel recusal provisions.
Notes: Read with section 455 and proposal-specific neutral replacement rules; legislative counsel should confirm that expedited procedures preserve a sufficient opportunity to raise disqualification.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 455 - Disqualification of justice judge or magistrate judge
Proposition Supported: Primary statutory source for judicial disqualification and recusal applied to JUD-011 and the independent REG-001 three-judge court.
Notes: Use as the baseline recusal rule; proposal-specific assignment and replacement language should supplement rather than narrow section 455.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 47 - Disqualification of trial judge to hear appeal
Proposition Supported: Primary statutory source barring a judge from hearing or determining an appeal from the decision of a case or issue tried by that judge, relevant because a D.C. Circuit judge may serve on the independent REG-001 three-judge trial court.
Notes: REG-001 expressly bars a circuit judge who served on the three-judge trial court from participating in appellate review; legislative counsel should confirm coordination with ordinary D.C. Circuit assignment and en banc practice.

Title or Description: Trump IRS and DOJ lawsuit report involving Acting Attorney General Blanche
Proposition Supported: User-supplied source lead concerning the July 13 court ruling on President Trump's IRS lawsuit, the attempted settlement, and DOJ involvement.
Notes: Automated retrieval was blocked; retained as the submitted source and cross-checked against AP and Reuters reporting before updating EMOL-015.

Title or Description: Judge blasts Trump's IRS lawsuit as filed for improper purpose and recommends attorney discipline
Proposition Supported: Supports the current manifestation that the district court treated the lawsuit as self-dealing, found an improper purpose, and referred attorney conduct for sanctions or discipline.

Title or Description: Justice Department Announces Anti-Weaponization Fund
Proposition Supported: Official DOJ announcement tying dismissal of President Trump's IRS suit and other claims to creation of a $1.776 billion fund to be financed from the Judgment Fund.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. District Court for the Southern District of Florida
Title or Description: Trump v. Internal Revenue Service - docket entry 106
Proposition Supported: Primary order finding no genuine adverseness or Article III case or controversy, finding improper purpose and bad faith, and imposing Rule 11 and inherent-authority sanctions.

Title or Description: Federal Rules of Civil Procedure - Rules 12 and 41
Proposition Supported: Primary procedural source for mandatory dismissal when subject-matter jurisdiction is absent and self-executing voluntary dismissal before service of an answer or summary-judgment motion, subject to an applicable federal statute.

Adjudicated 2026-07-18: Cite the official Rules 12 and 41 text supporting EMOL-015 threshold-screen and dismissal analysis. Integrated through areas/EMOL/research/EMOL-015-procedural-and-enforcement-analysis.md.

Title or Description: 26 U.S.C. § 7217 - Prohibition on executive branch influence over taxpayer audits and other investigations
Proposition Supported: Primary existing-law hook prohibiting specified executive actors from requesting an IRS audit or termination of an audit and requiring TIGTA reporting.
Notes: The current text does not expressly invalidate prospective audit-immunity terms and excludes the Attorney General from one category in the applicable-person definition; EMOL-015 proposes universal instrument-focused additions while retaining the existing request prohibition.

Title or Description: 26 U.S.C. § 7121 - Closing agreements
Proposition Supported: Primary authority for final written agreements resolving a person's Federal tax liability for a taxable period, subject to fraud, malfeasance, or material-misrepresentation limits.
Notes: Used to preserve lawful case-specific finality while distinguishing it from prospective immunity concerning unidentified matters or future taxable periods.

Title or Description: 26 U.S.C. § 7122 - Compromises
Proposition Supported: Primary authority for Treasury or DOJ compromise of identified civil or criminal tax cases and the associated record requirements.
Notes: Used to preserve lawful compromise of identified liabilities or cases without permitting restrictions on unidentified matters or future taxable periods.

Proposition Supported: Current manifestation describing an ICE fatal shooting the federal characterization that the vehicle was weaponized nearby security footage and unresolved questions concerning threat and justification.

Title or Description: 5 M.R.S. § 200-A - Criminal division

Title or Description: In re Neagle 135 U.S. 1
Proposition Supported: Primary Supreme Court source for Supremacy Clause protection of a federal officer performing federal duty and the necessary-and-proper factual inquiry.

Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 1442 - Federal officers or agencies sued or prosecuted

Title or Description: 18 U.S.C. § 4001 statutory note - Federal Law Enforcement Death in Custody Reporting Requirement

Title or Description: Goodyear Atomic Corp. v. Miller 486 U.S. 174

Title or Description: 5 U.S.C. § 102 - military departments

Notes: Definition hook for the standalone bill.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. House of Representatives Office of the Law Revision Counsel
Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 541 - United States attorneys
Proposition Supported: Provides for presidential nomination and Senate confirmation of United States attorneys and states that each United States attorney is subject to removal by the President.
Notes: Section 541(c) is the principal textual basis for treating immediate removal as legally supported rather than presumptively unlawful.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. House of Representatives Office of the Law Revision Counsel
Title or Description: 28 U.S.C. § 546 - Vacancies
Proposition Supported: Creates the Attorney General's 120-day interim appointment and the district court's authority to appoint a United States attorney after that period expires.
Notes: Read together with the Vacancies Act and section 541(c); section 546 does not expressly protect a judicial appointee from presidential removal.

Title or Description: Interim and Acting U.S. Attorneys Raise Open Legal Questions
Proposition Supported: Synthesizes the interaction among section 546 the Vacancies Act judicial appointments presidential removal and potential congressional amendments.
Notes: Official nonpartisan legal analysis; use the cited statutes opinions and OLC materials as primary authority for final conclusions.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the First Circuit
Title or Description: United States v. Hilario
Proposition Supported: Upholds judicial appointment of an interim United States attorney under section 546(d) and describes presidential removal and permanent appointment as checks on the court-appointed officer.
Notes: First Circuit authority rather than controlling Ninth Circuit precedent; relevant to the constitutional and statutory design analysis.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Department of Justice Office of Legal Counsel
Title or Description: Power to Remove Court-Appointed U.S. Attorneys
Proposition Supported: States the executive branch's longstanding view that the President may remove a court-appointed United States attorney.
Notes: Executive-branch legal interpretation rather than binding judicial precedent; assess alongside section 541(c) Hilario and current removal doctrine.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. House of Representatives Office of the Law Revision Counsel
Title or Description: 5 U.S.C. § 3349 - Reporting of vacancies
Proposition Supported: Requires executive agencies to report covered vacancy acting-service and nomination information to Congress and the Comptroller General and supplies the baseline for APPT-001's reporting amendments.
Notes: T1 should verify exact amendment placement reporting recipients deadlines and interaction with sections 3349a through 3349d.

Title or Description: The Vacancies Act: A Legal Overview
Proposition Supported: Explains current eligibility duration consequences reporting delegation and constitutional questions and identifies congressional options for amending the definition of functions and duties or limiting delegation.
Notes: Official nonpartisan legal synthesis; final claims should continue to rely on the statute and cited judicial authorities where available.

Title or Description: Federal Vacancies Reform Act
Proposition Supported: Collects GAO's statutory role vacancy reports violation letters and compliance decisions including current time-limit and delegation disputes.
Notes: Supports use of GAO as an existing accountability mechanism rather than creation of a new appointments commission.

Title or Description: Federal Workforce Data: Improvements Needed to Increase Transparency of Information on Senior Policy and Supporting Positions
Proposition Supported: Finds missing or inaccurate PLUM Reporting data and incomplete disclosure of limitations and recommends data-quality and public-use improvements.
Notes: Supports APPT-001's integrated public-record and data-quality direction; cost and implementation details require OPM and GAO workload review.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. House of Representatives Office of the Law Revision Counsel
Title or Description: 5 U.S.C. § 3330f - Governmentwide listing of senior positions
Proposition Supported: Establishes the PLUM Reporting website and required information concerning senior positions vacancies and acting officials.
Notes: Use with GAO-26-108164; legislative counsel should verify integration with the separate Vacancies Act reporting system.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. House of Representatives Office of the Law Revision Counsel
Title or Description: 5 U.S.C. § 3347 - Exclusivity and other temporary-service statutes
Proposition Supported: Makes the Vacancies Act generally exclusive while preserving statutes that expressly authorize acting service and addresses general delegation authority.
Notes: Central to APPT-001's anti-circumvention rule and its preservation of expressly authorized office-specific succession statutes.

Title or Description: Plyler v. Doe, 457 U.S. 202
Proposition Supported: Recognizes people physically present within a state including people unlawfully present as persons protected by due process and equal protection and explains that persons subject to the jurisdiction's civil and criminal obligations are not outside the protection of law.
Notes: Supports the candidate's constitutional personhood baseline; it does not establish identical scope for every right or every admission or extraterritorial context.

Title or Description: Alienage Classification
Proposition Supported: Summarizes Supreme Court doctrine recognizing lawful unlawful temporary and permanent noncitizens as persons within the Equal Protection Clause while identifying alienage-classification doctrine.
Notes: Official legal synthesis; use controlling opinions for final doctrinal propositions. Adjudicated 2026-07-18: Official constitutional synthesis supports the neutral person-based equal-protection baseline while preserving claim-specific limits. Integrated through framework/logs/HORIZON_SCAN_LOG.md.

Title or Description: Exclusion and Removal of Non-U.S. Nationals
Proposition Supported: Explains the distinction between initial admission and the due-process protections recognized for noncitizens physically present in the United States.
Notes: Supports precise claim-specific boundaries without treating immigration status as a general absence of constitutional protection. Adjudicated 2026-07-18: Official constitutional synthesis supports the admission-versus-presence due-process distinction used by the candidate. Integrated through framework/logs/HORIZON_SCAN_LOG.md.

Title or Description: S.J.Res. 14 (117th Congress), Public Law 117-23
Proposition Supported: Congress used the Congressional Review Act to nullify EPA's September 2020 oil-and-gas methane rule after the D.C. Circuit denied interim relief and expedited litigation.
Notes: Retained as a rejected JUD-012 comparator showing that the challenged rule remained subject to ordinary litigation and an express legislative mechanism.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia via Justia
Title or Description: Does 1-9 v. Department of Justice - Memorandum Opinion
Proposition Supported: Dismisses disclosure and retaliation claims as too speculative after jurisdictional discovery found no evidence that disclosure or adverse action was imminent; supplies a comparator rather than proof that threatened retaliation occurred.
Notes: Reader-facing treatment: areas/DOJ/issues/DOJ-002.md (Annotation). Use with care: the opinion addresses standing and pleading on the record before the court and does not establish that political review of investigators would be lawful if concrete adverse action occurred.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. District Court for the District of Alaska via Justia
Title or Description: Gwich'in Steering Committee v. Bernhardt - Order Denying Preliminary Injunction
Proposition Supported: Denied pre-sale relief because the record of decision authorized no immediate on-the-ground activity and the court expected to decide the merits before immediate environmental injury, while acknowledging possible longer-term injury.
Notes: A rejected JUD-012 comparator: the order found no immediate physical harm and did not make later merits review unavailable.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit via Justia
Title or Description: Ramos v. Wolf, 975 F.3d 872
Date: Sept. 14 2020; vacated for rehearing en banc; reviewed July 16 2026
Proposition Supported: The panel treated the TPS statute as barring APA review of termination determinations while reaching the constitutional claim; the opinion was later vacated and therefore is not current precedential authority.
Notes: Retained only to document the source lead; Mullin v. Doe is the current controlling JUD-012 and RIGHTS-002 source.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fourth Circuit via Justia
Title or Description: Wild Virginia v. Council on Environmental Quality, 56 F.4th 281
Proposition Supported: Affirms that the organizations' facial challenge to the 2020 NEPA rule lacked a ripe or sufficiently concrete application-specific injury while leaving later challenges to agency applications available.
Notes: A rejected JUD-012 comparator because later application-specific review remained available.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California via Justia
Title or Description: California v. Wheeler - Order Denying Preliminary Relief
Proposition Supported: Denied preliminary relief against the Navigable Waters Protection Rule after assessing likelihood of success and the other interim-relief factors; later proceedings became moot after remand and a separate court vacated the rule.
Notes: A rejected JUD-012 comparator because an ordinary preliminary-relief denial is not review foreclosure.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit via Justia
Title or Description: Northern Alaska Environmental Center v. Department of the Interior, 965 F.3d 705
Proposition Supported: Affirms the challenged lease-sale analysis on the merits and treats one contention as waived; it is not a case in which threshold doctrine left executive authority wholly untested.
Notes: A rejected JUD-012 comparator because the court adjudicated the challenged agency analysis on the merits.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia via Federal Election Commission
Title or Description: Democratic National Committee v. Trump - Memorandum Opinion
Proposition Supported: Dismisses the pre-enforcement challenge to Executive Order 14215 for lack of standing and ripeness after executive and FEC counsel represented that the order had not been and was not planned to be used to control the FEC's interpretation of FECA.
Notes: Reader-facing treatment: areas/REG/issues/REG-001.md (Annotation). Monitor for concrete application. The court emphasized the grave election risk if FEC independence were compromised and stated that changed circumstances could support a later challenge; the opinion did not approve presidential control of FEC legal judgment.

Title or Description: Little Sisters of the Poor Saints Peter and Paul Home v. Pennsylvania, 591 U.S. 657
Proposition Supported: Held that the governing statute gave the agencies broad authority to create contraceptive-coverage exemptions and that the rules satisfied the challenged APA procedural requirements.
Notes: Reader-facing treatment: areas/RIGHTS/issues/RIGHTS-001.md (Annotation). Establishes a lawful-policy-change boundary: statutory delegation and reasoned rulemaking matter even when civil-rights or benefit administration changes materially.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia via Justia
Title or Description: National Fair Housing Alliance v. Carson - Memorandum Opinion
Proposition Supported: Dismissed for lack of standing and concluded that HUD adequately explained withdrawal of a local-government fair-housing assessment tool while the statutory duty to affirmatively further fair housing remained.
Notes: Reader-facing treatment: areas/RIGHTS/issues/RIGHTS-001.md (Manifestations). Illustrates executive discretion over the administrative method used to carry out a continuing civil-rights duty without establishing that the withdrawal was unlawful.

Title or Description: Trump Administration Finalizes Endangered Species Act Regulation Reform

Notes: Primary source for the agency's description of the finalized action. It does not by itself establish the final rule text's legal effect or an institutional defect; retained for HOR-038 monitoring.

Authority / Publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel
Title or Description: 16 U.S.C. § 1533 — Determination of endangered species and threatened species
Proposition Supported: Section 4(b)(1)(A) makes listing determinations solely on the best scientific and commercial data available, while section 4(b)(2) permits consideration of economic, national-security, and other relevant impacts in critical-habitat designations and exclusions subject to an extinction safeguard.
Notes: Controls the statutory distinction that defines HOR-038's monitoring boundary. The candidate is not a finding that either category of 2026 regulation exceeds delegated authority.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fourth Circuit
Title or Description: Tun-Cos v. Perrotte, 922 F.3d 514
Proposition Supported: Primary manifestation in which alleged completed ICE searches and seizures received no constitutional merits adjudication because no Bivens cause of action was available and immigration procedures often did not redress such violations.
Notes: Anchor for the absent federal-officer remedy category; immigration and civil-rights records retain the underlying rights questions.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit
Title or Description: Meshal v. Higgenbotham, 804 F.3d 417
Proposition Supported: Primary manifestation in which the court and parties recognized that a citizen alleging overseas federal detention and coercive interrogation had no alternative remedy but declined to recognize a Bivens action.
Notes: Anchor for completed constitutional injury with no alternative process; national-security and extraterritorial boundaries require further review.

Title or Description: Hernández v. Mesa, 589 U.S. 93
Proposition Supported: Primary manifestation in which the Court declined to extend Bivens after a cross-border fatal shooting and did not decide the underlying constitutional question after DOJ declined prosecution and the United States denied extradition.
Notes: JUD-012 owns the absent judicial remedy; DOM-005 retains the critical-incident and evidence-access cross-reference.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fourth Circuit
Title or Description: El-Masri v. United States, 479 F.3d 296
Proposition Supported: Primary state-secrets manifestation in which extraordinary-rendition allegations could not proceed to merits adjudication because protected information was indispensable.
Notes: JUD-012 owns the review foreclosure; WAR-001 retains the overseas counterterrorism-policy cross-reference.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit
Title or Description: Mohamed v. Jeppesen Dataplan, Inc., 614 F.3d 1070
Proposition Supported: Primary state-secrets manifestation in which rendition-related contractor liability could not be litigated without an unacceptable risk of disclosing protected information.
Notes: JUD-012 owns the review foreclosure; WAR-001 retains the overseas counterterrorism-policy cross-reference.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fourth Circuit
Title or Description: Wikimedia Foundation v. National Security Agency
Proposition Supported: Primary state-secrets manifestation in which a genuine factual dispute relevant to standing could not proceed because protected surveillance information was indispensable.
Notes: JUD-012 owns the review barrier; surveillance records retain the underlying authority and deployment questions.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fourth Circuit
Title or Description: Abilt v. Central Intelligence Agency, 848 F.3d 305
Proposition Supported: Primary non-rendition state-secrets example in which a covert employee's disability-discrimination and retaliation claims could not be litigated because protected information was necessary to both sides.
Notes: Use to test whether the state-secrets effect extends beyond surveillance and overseas counterterrorism.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit
Title or Description: Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington v. Trump, 924 F.3d 602
Proposition Supported: Primary manifestation of limits on judicial supervision of day-to-day presidential compliance with the Presidential Records Act.
Notes: JUD-012 owns the review-access question; REC-001 retains records preservation custody and Archives enforcement.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit via Justia
Title or Description: Montanans for Multiple Use v. Barbouletos, 568 F.3d 225
Proposition Supported: Primary manifestation of a circuit holding that the Congressional Review Act's review bar prevented judicial enforcement of an alleged rule-submission failure.
Notes: Further research must assess circuit disagreement and whether other challenges to the underlying rule provide an adequate substitute.

Title or Description: Thryv, Inc. v. Click-to-Call Technologies, LP, 590 U.S. 45
Proposition Supported: Primary specialized-review-bar example in which courts could review patentability but not whether the agency lawfully instituted the proceeding despite an alleged statutory time bar.
Notes: Use to distinguish claim-specific statutory review foreclosure from complete absence of all judicial review.

Title or Description: United States v. Fausto, 484 U.S. 439
Proposition Supported: Primary historical example in which an employee excluded from the Civil Service Reform Act review path was also denied alternative Back Pay Act review.
Notes: Congress's later civil-service amendment supplies a historical repair comparator.

Title or Description: Public Law 94-574, amendment to 5 U.S.C. § 702
Proposition Supported: Official historical repair removing sovereign immunity as a general barrier to nonmonetary judicial review of federal administrative action.
Notes: Use as a model of Congress repairing a cross-cutting review obstacle without altering Article III.

Title or Description: Veterans' Judicial Review Act of 1988, Public Law 100-687
Proposition Supported: Official historical repair creating independent judicial review of veterans-benefit decisions after a longstanding review exclusion.
Notes: Use as an alternative-review-institution model; later development should assess administration and cost comparators.

Title or Description: H.R. 6091 - Bivens Act of 2025
Proposition Supported: Direct current proposal to extend 42 U.S.C. § 1983 to persons acting under federal authority and create a statutory civil remedy for constitutional violations.
Notes: Direct comparator for only the federal-officer cause-of-action component; it does not resolve state-secrets or other review barriers.

Title or Description: The State Secrets Privilege: National Security Information in Civil Litigation
Proposition Supported: Doctrine map for assertion review effects displacement unresolved questions examples and congressional options concerning the state-secrets privilege.
Notes: Use as a research map and verify publication-ready case propositions against controlling opinions and legislative text.

Title or Description: Trump administration orders ICE to suspend most vehicle stops after 2 deadly shootings
Proposition Supported: Reports the Biddeford and Houston vehicle-stop shootings the non-target status of the drivers and the temporary internal direction concerning most vehicle stops.
Notes: Use for the reported operational sequence and policy response only; official findings remain pending and the report does not establish whether either stop or use of force was unlawful.

Authority / Publisher: Maine Office of the Attorney General
Title or Description: Federal Law Enforcement Fatal Use of Deadly Force in Biddeford Maine July 13 2026
Proposition Supported: Official State statement identifying the participating agencies initial federal account and Maine Attorney General investigative posture.
Notes: The statement reports an initial account and an ongoing investigation; it is not a final factual or legal finding.

Title or Description: United States v. Brignoni-Ponce 422 U.S. 873
Proposition Supported: Controlling immigration-stop baseline requiring reasonable suspicion based on specific articulable facts and rejecting apparent ancestry alone as sufficient.
Notes: Use as the constitutional baseline; later development must account for subsequent doctrine and the facts of each stop.

Title or Description: 8 C.F.R. § 287.8 - Standards for enforcement activities
Proposition Supported: Current regulatory standards for immigration questioning warrantless arrest and related enforcement conduct.
Notes: Verify the current effective text and any amendments during future candidate review.

Title or Description: Vehicle Stops Policy issued through the Castañon Nava settlement

Notes: The settlement and policy's current nationwide status implementation and any attempted rescission require verification before treating it as presently binding.

Title or Description: Second amended complaint in State of Colorado v. Trump

EPA: The court found that the EPA articulated a satisfactory explanation for the levels set, based on scientific evidence that levels at the top end of the range might not adequately protect against adverse health effects. Further, the EPA did not act arbitrarily in drawing conclusions from uncertain and conflicting data. In setting the "margin of safety," the EPA could err on the side of overprotection. The EPA was not permitted to consider costs such as the health consequences of unemployment in promulgating national ambient air quality standards. The association did not have standing to challenge the EPA's alleged failure to furnish adequate control techniques information to the states under 42 U.S.C.S. Â§ 7408(b)(1) and (c). The court did not have jurisdiction to order the EPA to take action on a fine particulate standard relating to visibility impairment because the EPA had not yet taken final action.
